title: Chat kind-discriminated optional fields have no type-system enforcement or comment
description: The Chat struct has fields tagged with comments like '--- direct only ---' and '--- group only ---', but the type system does not enforce these constraints. A contributor constructing a Chat (e.g. in tests) can populate group-only fields on a direct Chat without any warning. There is also no comment explaining that Chat is a deserialization-only type and that invalid field combinations can only arrive from a buggy server. Without this context, a contributor might assume Chat is also used as a client-side construction type. Add a type doc comment stating: 'This type is deserialization-only. Field applicability by kind is enforced by the server, not by this struct.'
